prompt: A guest asks: 'Does the Caesar salad have anchovies?'

choices.feedback: Correct — classic Caesar dressing is anchovy-based.
Incorrect — could cause an allergy issue.

prompt: Guest's steak came out well-done instead of medium-rare, visibly annoyed.

choices.text: Apologize, remake immediately, check in after
choices.feedback: Best response — acknowledges mistake and fixes it fast.
Avoid blaming the kitchen to the guest — just fix it.
Risks escalation and a bad review.

prompt: A guest asks: 'Is the mushroom risotto gluten-free?'

choices.text: Yes, it's rice-based with no gluten ingredients
choices.feedback: Correct — arborio rice, stock, and parmesan are all gluten-free.
Incorrect — risotto is made with rice, not barley.

prompt: A guest says their table has been waiting 20 minutes with no drink order taken.

choices.text: Apologize sincerely, take the order now, and offer to comp an item
choices.feedback: Best response — owns the delay and offers a real gesture of goodwill.

choices.text: Explain that the restaurant is short-staffed today
choices.feedback: The guest doesn't need an excuse — they need to feel prioritized now.

choices.text: Tell them another server was assigned to their table
choices.feedback: Passing blame internally doesn't help the guest feel cared for.
